title: Pre recorded Lecture Part A Slides
body:
Dr Linh Nguyen, Course Convenor Lecturer UNSW Centre for Social Impact Email: linh-nguyen@unsw.edu.au  ▪ Project management refers to the application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project tasks in order to fulfill project requirements. ▪ It includes processes of planning, organizing, securing, leading, and managing resources effectively to accomplish specific objectives within defined timeframes, budgets, and constraints Image: Stanford University

Source: Turner (2014); Portny and Portny (2022)

Consulting is the practice of providing expert advice and services to organizations in addressing complex business challenges and improving performance (Deelmann 2019)

Consultants help organizations improve their performance, solve problems, and achieve their strategic goals through Identifying problems and opportunities, conducting research and analysis, co-creating devise actionable improvement strategies (Institute of Management Consultants Australia; Bain & Company). Consultants can specialize in various areas, such as management, finance, marketing, operations, etc.

In this unit, your group acts as a consulting team specializing in social entrepreneurship. Each enterprise presents unique challenges, requiring consultants to assess their business model, organizational structure, and core competencies. The industry partner/ client expects you to provide valuable advice on addressing their challenges and enhancing their operation and social impact.

https://project-management.com   CLEARLY DEFINED DOCUMENTATION MEASUREMENT OF PROJECT DELIVERABLES SUCCESS

▪ Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) ▪ Software Tools for WBS ▪ Gantt Chart ▪ Resource Allocation and Timeframes Image: https://venngage.com/

Task implementation: • Executing project tasks according to the approved plan • Following established processes and standards • Data Collection • Data Analysis

Resource management • Allocating and managing project resources (human, material, etc) • Optimizing resource utilization and addressing resource conflicts

Team Management, Communication and collaboration • Fostering effective collaboration, communication, and adherence to processes within the project team to ensure optimal productivity and synergy. • Maintaining open communication channels all parties • Conducting regular meetings and sharing project updates

Performance Progress Tracking Risk Management Measurement

Change Control Status Reporting Adjustment

Delivering and presenting results to clients/ Project Closure Deliverables and Handover: • Clearly outline the final deliverables and their status. Explain the handover process for completed products, documentation, and knowledge transfer. • Review and ensure that all project deliverables have been completed and meet the specified requirements Clarity and Comprehension: Consultants must articulate recommendations in a clear, concise manner to facilitate comprehension and foster meaningful dialogue. Tailored Solutions to Client Needs: • Clarity and Comprehension • Tailored Solutions

Social Social Enterprise Enterprise Mission Scale

Social Social Enterprise Enterprise Relation to Resources Government

SAFETY? TOGETHER! IT’S BRAVE TO BE VULNERABLE START SLOW TO WORK FAST

Overcoming defensiveness is only Vulnerability is the birthplace of belonging, joy, First build relations, and only then seek and possible if we co-regulate together. courage, empathy and creativity. find solutions. Complexity needs multiple perspectives.

▪ Bain & Company https://www.bain.com/careers/work-with-us/what-is-managementconsulting/ ▪ Deelmann, T. (2019) ‘Consulting: Characterization of Concepts and Connections’, in Nissen, V. (ed.) Advances in Consulting Research. Contributions to Management Science. Cham: Springer. ▪ Institute of Management Consultants Australia https://imc.org.au/ ▪ https://project-management.com ▪ Portny, SE & Portny, JL 2022, Project management 6th ed., John Wiley & Sons, Inc., Hoboken, New Jersey. ▪ Turner, JR 2014, The Handbook of project-based management : leading strategic change in organizations Fourth edition., McGraw-Hill Education, New York, New York.

THANK YOU!
